title: Flatten a nested call into a named local
note: lifts each inner call out of its argument slot into a
, so every call reads on its own line. Simplicity is AST depth — one nested expression becomes two shallow statements.

title: Set an if-test slot to a new expression
note: sets an if-statement's test slot to a new expression — the condition
becomes
. The two-sided guard that refuses a statement (the paired rejection) accepts these: arg 1 is an if-statement, arg 2 an expression.

title: Set a return statement's argument
note: sets a return statement's argument slot to a new expression — here
becomes
. It is the same guarded slot-setter shape as the if-test setter, on a different node; the two-sided guard it carries is the one
installs.

title: Replace an identifier with an expression, everywhere in the fn
note: swaps every occurrence of an identifier for a parsed
— not just a name, so
becomes
at both sites. Unlike a rename, the replacement can be any expression.

title: Remove an unused import
note: drops only imports no code references —
is imported but never called, so its line goes;
stays because
uses it. This is the remove-only third of what
does.

title: Canonicalize import paths
note: rewrites every import to its canonical repo path — here
becomes
— adding and removing nothing. It is the canonicalize third of
, shown alone.

title: A single-item helper refuses a two-item list
note: Exactly-one is an invariant, not a hope:
asserts its list has one item (via
) before returning it, so a two-item list is refused at the boundary — the same guard
leans on to require a single-export file.
expectText: throws — a single list has exactly one item

title: A slot setter refuses a statement in an expression slot
note: The if-test slot holds an expression; a statement in it is refused by the two-sided guard (
fails on arg 1).
expectText: throws — arg 1 is not an expression node

title: A frozen app is refused for prod build, promote, and delete
note: throws for any app on
, guarding every path that would change or remove its production assets. Non-frozen apps pass.
expectText: throws — replace is on the frozen list

title: Repair imports across a whole multi-function file
note: A migration file can hold many declarations; program-wide repair adds every missing repo import — single-export
would fail here. Locally-declared names (
) are never self-imported.

title: Auto-imports: add missing, remove unused, canonicalize paths
note: composes all three import verbs —
(used, unimported) is added,
(imported, unused) is removed,
(wrong
path) is canonicalized to
. Provably auto-safe: idempotent, total, state-independent.

title: Add a two-sided guard to a slot setter
note: prepends
and repairs imports; one predicate per parameter.

title: Generate a node-type predicate from scratch
note: creates a new
predicate;
is empty because nothing existed. Runs file-side (sandboxed) — see the app or the temp-file runner.

title: Rename a function across every file that uses it
note: moves the definition and repoints every reference in one operation — the call sites in
, its
, and the file name itself all change together, so the rename is behavior-preserving by construction.

before.name: app_x.mjs

after.name: app_x.mjs

title: Delete a function only after proving nothing uses it
note: scans every file for a reference before removing one —
never calls
, so the file is safe to delete. If any file used it, the delete is refused instead.

title: Refuse to delete a function that is still used
note: finds
referenced in
, so it refuses — the same scan that made the previous delete safe stops this one. The file stays.

before.name: app_z.mjs

title: Fold every occurrence, not just the first
note: loops the single-occurrence
to fixpoint. Here
's atomized body —
then
— appears twice in F, so both blocks collapse into calls. Complete-on-a-function, not one-shot.

title: Discover and fold the matching fn — without naming which
note: is handed a set of candidate fns (here
and
) and folds whichever ones already match a block of F. It finds that the hand-written
then
IS
and folds it;
matches nothing and is left alone. This is the fix for reinventing a fn you did not know existed.

title: Refuse to fold when an internal local escapes the block
note: Folding collapses the matched block into one opaque call, deleting its internal locals. Here
is used again after the block (
), so the call would delete a value F still needs.
refuses — after equals before, nothing changed.

title: Refuse to fold across a gap between the matched statements
note: The MVP folds only a CONTIGUOUS block — nothing moves, so the fold is sound without a purity oracle. Here an unrelated
sits between
and
, so no contiguous window matches and
declines. Gapped folds need side-effect commutativity and are deferred.

title: Fold hand-written wrap logic into a call to the pure fn
note: (run as
) recognises
's atomized body —
then
— appearing inline in F and collapses it into one call. This is the opposite of inlining: never author the same logic twice.

title: Property names are not references, so they need no import
note: A name in a property or key position is text, not a variable.
is a real function here, so
and
used to pull in an import nothing referenced — and because the add ran first, nothing ever removed it. Only
is genuinely called, so only it is imported.

title: Shorthand and computed keys are references, so they keep their import
note: The mirror of the property-name case, and the one that would bite hardest if the rule went too far:
and
both read the value of
, so dropping its import here would break the file at runtime. The import stays.
